mitre.name: MITRE ATT&CK
mitre.description: Knowledge base of adversary tactics and techniques

T1190.name: Exploit Public-Facing Application
T1190.description: Attackers exploit vulnerabilities in internet-facing applications

T1059.name: Command and Scripting Interpreter
T1059.description: Abuse command and script interpreters to execute commands

T1071.name: Application Layer Protocol
T1071.description: Use application layer protocols for C2 communication

T1486.name: Data Encrypted for Impact
T1486.description: Encrypt data on systems to interrupt availability (ransomware)

T1566.name: Phishing
T1566.description: Send phishing messages to gain access

T1078.name: Valid Accounts
T1078.description: Use stolen credentials to gain initial access

cis.name: CIS Critical Security Controls
cis.description: Prioritized set of actions for cyber defense

1.name: Inventory and Control of Enterprise Assets
1.description: Actively manage all enterprise assets connected to infrastructure

2.name: Inventory and Control of Software Assets
2.description: Actively manage all software on the network

6.name: Access Control Management
6.description: Use processes and tools to track/control access to assets

8.name: Audit Log Management
8.description: Collect, manage, and analyze audit logs

10.name: Malware Defenses
10.description: Control installation, spread, and execution of malware

13.name: Network Monitoring and Defense
13.description: Operate processes and tooling to establish defenses

identify.name: Identify
identify.description: Develop understanding of managing cybersecurity risk

detect.name: Detect
detect.description: Develop and implement activities to identify cybersecurity events

respond.name: Respond
respond.description: Develop and implement activities to take action

recover.name: Recover
recover.description: Develop and implement activities to restore capabilities

iso27001.name: ISO/IEC 27001
iso27001.description: International standard for information security management

killchain.name: Cyber Kill Chain
killchain.description: Phases of a cyber attack (Lockheed Martin)

phases.description: Research, identification and selection of targets
Coupling exploit with backdoor into deliverable payload
phases.example: Creating malicious document with embedded exploit

phases.description: Transmission of weapon to targeted environment
phases.example: Email attachment, USB drive, compromised website
Buffer overflow, SQL injection, zero-day exploit

phases.description: Installation of malware on target system

risks.name: Broken Access Control
risks.description: Restrictions on what authenticated users can do not properly enforced

risks.name: Cryptographic Failures
risks.description: Failures related to cryptography leading to sensitive data exposure
risks.example: Transmitting data in clear text, weak algorithms

risks.name: Injection
risks.description: Hostile data sent to an interpreter as part of command or query
risks.example: SQL injection, NoSQL injection, OS command injection

risks.name: Insecure Design
risks.example: Lack of rate limiting, weak authentication flows

risks.name: Identification and Authentication Failures
risks.description: Confirmation of user identity, authentication fails

risks.name: Software and Data Integrity Failures
risks.description: Code and infrastructure not protected against integrity violations

risks.name: Security Logging and Monitoring Failures
risks.example: Not logging login failures, delayed detection

risks.name: Server-Side Request Forgery (SSRF)
risks.description: Fetching remote resources without validating user-supplied URL

certifications.name: CISSP
certifications.description: Gold standard for security professionals, covers 8 domains

certifications.name: CISM
certifications.description: For security managers, focuses on governance and risk

certifications.name: CEH
certifications.description: Learn to think like a hacker, penetration testing skills

certifications.name: Security+
certifications.description: Foundation level, great for SOC analysts starting out
